channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: Destiny fans petition for Destiny 3, with heartfelt messages
item.content:encoded:
A petition for Destiny 3 has garnered over 48,000 signatures in a very short time, with many explaining why the game means so much to them.

“The desire for new adventures, fresh storylines, and innovative gameplay features is palpable among players everywhere. Destiny 3 would not only meet these demands but also expand the horizons of what is possible within this universe,” reads the petition.  “Our passion and commitment to the Destiny franchise drives us to reach out to Sony and express our collective voice. We believe in the potential of Destiny 3 to inspire new generations of gamers and to keep the fire of the Guardian spirit alive.”

Along with signatures, there are many comments explaining why the Destiny franchise has meant so much to the players.

“I have played Destiny since the Alpha in 2014, I have been here for every expansion, usually buying the most expensive version so I could get all the content once it came out. This franchise has helped me make friends that I never would have met, and got me through really tough times in my life. “

“This is the only game I have ever loved. Destiny 2 got me through some of the darkest times in my life. This game brought me countless friends and memories. I am incredibly sad about this, and haven’t figured out how to put the right words together. I just don’t want Destiny to die.”

“My greatest gaming experiences came from playing raids, dungeons, and campaigns. Irreplaceable moments and just pure fun with my girlfriend I was 19 years old when I started Destiny; I’m now 29. Destiny has been part of my life for over a decade and wish the same for the next generation.”

“This game has been a major part of my entire life and has always been a positive constant throughout every major change I’ve gone through in my life. To see it end like this is heart-breaking.”<

“Destiny growing up was home to me and helped me get through the roughest childhood growing up.”

“Destiny has been such a big part of my life. It came out when I was 14 years old and I have played it through high school, college, getting married, having kids and I can’t imagine a world without more stories and new adventures in Sol.”

You get the picture; there are hundreds more similar comments.

Bungie’s message about the end of the game has not helped; they admitted all the mistakes that fans have been telling them for years, most notably that The Final Shape was the perfect ending and D3 should have followed. Instead Bungie implemented a raft of changes, including side-lining the Director and replacing it with Portal which worked more like something from a mobile game, with seemingly endless tasks which required messing about with modifiers just to get small rewards. It was hated by almost every player.

At the moment, it seems that Destiny 3 is not in development.  As someone pointed out in the Petition comments, Sony made a big move into Live Service games, most of which have failed or been scrapped, and yet they the one huge franchise they had, the one that people loved,  they have run in to the ground with terrible management decisions and will not continue.

Source: Change.org



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/05/23/destiny-fans-petition-for-destiny-3-with-heartfelt-messages/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Something for the Bank Holiday Weekend – 23/05/26
item.content:encoded:
Another bank holiday is upon us, and while it’s the last one for a few months, it’s set to be a scorcher in the UK. Time to find out where you stashed the sun screen if you’re heading out, remember to stay hydrated, get the fans going and operate a strict window opening/closing and blinds policy to try to keep your home cool.

Also, play games and have a barbecue.

In the News This Week

It’s worrying times for Bungie right now with the end of Destiny 2 development. But there was also one of the first mid-year game celebrations with the 10th Warhammer Skulls.

  • Destiny 2’s next season will be its last – One final Monument of Triumph as the Director returns
  • Report: Major layoffs to hit Bungie and Destiny 3 is not in development
  • PlayStation State of Play returns on 2nd June with Wolverine for Summer Game Fest
  • Warhorse confirms both a The Lord of the Rings RPG and more Kingdom Come are in development
  • Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate – Deathwatch sequel announced for PC, PS5 & Xbox Series X|S
  • Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Deathmaster is a sneaky Skaven stealth platformer
  • Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War IV is coming out in September, post-launch plans revealed
  • F1 25: 2026 Season Pack DLC launches in June – will you go mad for MADRING?
  • Embracer is splitting in half again, sending Fellowship Entertainment on its merry way
  • Sony’s single player games will no longer be releasing on PC

Games in Review & Featured Articles

A little lighter on the reviews front this week, but keep an eye out for our LEGO Batman verdict next week.

  • Outbound – PS5, XSX|S, NSW2, NSW, PC – 7/10
  • Yoshi and the Mysterious Book – NSW2 – 7/10 (Video Review)
  • Duck Side of the Moon – PC – 7/10
  • Kumitantei: Old School Slaughter Episode 1 – PC – 6/10
  • The Last Ninja Collection – NSW, PC – 5/10

Alongside this, Steve gave some thoughts on the Early Access release of Deadhaus Sonata, which is a spiritual Blood Omen sequel, and Dom put a (fitness) ring on it – the Luna Smart Ring Gen 2.

It was far busier in the previews, though. I went hands on with Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, and had a great time revisiting one of the most popular AC titles with a fresh lick of paint, some revised gameplay, and just the right amount of 2010s AC jank.

I also went hands on with The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales, which has an unassuming title for a great feeling HD-2D Zelda-like, and dipped into the Boltgun 2 demo for Warhammer Skulls. Jason joined me on demo duty with Warhammer Survivors.

Dom was at Digital Dragons in Poland, and had some thoughts about this year’s event and its showcase of Polish game development. This also led to previews of Pit Panic and Mutter, with more to come.

Finishing up for the week, What We Played featured Forza Horizon 6, Lego Batman & Digital Dragons.

Trailer Park

Let’s go all Warhammer Skulls for Trailer Park this week.

The Warhammer 40,000 Dark Heresy closed beta is now on

Total War: Warhammer 40,000 is teasing a return to Armageddon

The Skitarii are the next new class for Darktide

Speed Freeks is out now on console with a bunch of new features

That’s the round up for this week. Hope you have a great weekend and we’ll see you back here next week!



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/05/23/something-for-the-bank-holiday-weekend-23-05-26/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
